toggle.note: All 6 OMOP table names are identical in synthetic and real modes. No query changes required to switch between them.

tables.name: person
tables.role: One row per patient. Key columns: person_id (PK), year_of_birth, gender_source_value, birth_date.

tables.name: condition_occurrence
tables.role: Breast cancer diagnoses. condition_source_value = 'Malignant neoplasm of breast' identifies breast cancer patients.

tables.name: measurement
tables.role: Structured biomarker results (ER/PR/HER2). measurement_source_value identifies the marker; value_source_value = 'Positive'/'Negative'. Absent for notes-only patients (person_ids 181-240).

tables.name: observation
tables.role: Menopausal status (observation_source_value='Menopausal status', value_source_value='Postmenopausal'/'Premenopausal') and AJCC tumor stage (observation_source_value='AJCC stage', value_source_value in Stage I..Stage IV). Each patient gets both observation types.

tables.name: drug_exposure
tables.role: Prior therapy. drug_source_value='Trastuzumab' or 'Pertuzumab' flags prior anti-HER2 therapy — disqualifier for Trial A. Present for person_ids 21-30 (ineligible controls).

tables.name: note
tables.role: Free-text pathology reports in note_text. Contains biomarker status phrasing for both-agree patients (person_ids 1-180) and notes-only patients (181-240). Absent biomarker phrasing for structured-only patients (241-300). The NLP value story: ~60 patients (181-240) are invisible to structured queries but discoverable via note_text extraction.

trial_a.name: HER2-Positive Breast Cancer
trial_a.ineligible_reason: Prior anti-HER2 therapy (Trastuzumab and/or Pertuzumab in drug_exposure)
criteria: NO drug_exposure row with drug_source_value IN ('Trastuzumab','Pertuzumab')

both_agree.description: Structured AND note biomarkers present. NLP ground truth for MLflow evaluation.

notes_only.description: Biomarker status in note_text ONLY — invisible to structured queries. The NLP value story: ~18 Trial A eligible, ~15 Trial B eligible recoverable only via NLP extraction.

structured_only.description: Structured measurement rows only; note_text has no biomarker phrasing.

ajcc_stage.distribution_note: 15% Stage I / 28% IIA / 22% IIB / 18% IIIA / 10% IIIB / 7% IV

gold.note: This demo has no SDP pipeline — all 6 tables are written directly by the data generator. They behave as 'gold' (analytics-ready, OMOP CDM compliant).

dependency_map.note: both-agree patients as NLP evaluation ground truth

grain_constraints.note: One row per patient, 300 rows total
Up to 3 rows per patient (ER, PR, HER2); absent for notes-only group (person_ids 181-240)
2 rows per patient: menopausal status + AJCC stage

source_inputs.description: Faker-based Python script writes all 6 OMOP tables. Parameters: argv[1]=catalog, argv[2]=schema. Seed=42 for determinism.
Customer's real OMOP CDM tables. Must have identical schema to the synthetic tables (exact FH curated_omop.omop column names and types).

verify_queries.description: Row counts for all 6 OMOP tables — all must be > 0 after data generation
verify_queries.sql: SELECT 'person' AS tbl, COUNT(*) AS n FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.person UNION ALL SELECT 'condition_occurrence', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.condition_occurrence UNION ALL SELECT 'measurement', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.measurement UNION ALL SELECT 'observation', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.observation UNION ALL SELECT 'drug_exposure', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.drug_exposure UNION ALL SELECT 'note', COUNT(*) FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.note ORDER BY tbl

verify_queries.description: Trial A eligible patients (HER2+, breast cancer dx, no anti-HER2 prior therapy)
verify_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT m.person_id) AS n FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.measurement m JOIN ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.condition_occurrence co ON m.person_id = co.person_id AND co.condition_source_value = 'Malignant neoplasm of breast' WHERE m.measurement_source_value = 'HER2/neu' AND m.value_source_value = 'Positive' AND m.person_id NOT IN (SELECT person_id FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.drug_exposure WHERE drug_source_value IN ('Trastuzumab','Pertuzumab'))

verify_queries.description: Trial B eligible patients (ER+/HER2-, postmenopausal, breast cancer dx)
verify_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT m_er.person_id) AS n FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.measurement m_er JOIN ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.measurement m_her2 ON m_er.person_id = m_her2.person_id AND m_her2.measurement_source_value = 'HER2/neu' AND m_her2.value_source_value = 'Negative' JOIN ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.observation obs ON m_er.person_id = obs.person_id AND obs.observation_source_value = 'Menopausal status' AND obs.value_source_value = 'Postmenopausal' WHERE m_er.measurement_source_value = 'Estrogen receptor' AND m_er.value_source_value = 'Positive'

verify_queries.description: Patients with pathology notes but no structured biomarker measurements (the NLP value story)
verify_queries.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT n.person_id) AS n FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.note n WHERE n.person_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT person_id FROM ${client_catalog}.${client_schema}.measurement WHERE measurement_source_value IN ('HER2/neu','Estrogen receptor','Progesterone receptor'))

lock_targets.reason: OMOP CDM table names (person, condition_occurrence, measurement, observation, drug_exposure, note) are standard. The synth/real toggle works because synthetic and real OMOP tables share identical names. Renaming breaks the toggle.
Adaptation skill must not modify itself
